title: Rewriting My Family's Money Story
subtitle: A journal prompt set for naming what you are changing in your family's relationship with money and claiming the turning point
excerpt: A four-prompt journal set from Day 77 of a 12-week money programme. The prompts move from imagining what a rewritten family money story would say, to sitting with the fact that you are the one changing it, to finding the specific strength in your ancestry that made this possible.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 77 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when the weight of the inherited money story is heavier than the new one, or when you want to mark and hold the sense of being the person through whom it changes.

content.text: A four-prompt journal set from Day 77 of a 12-week money programme. Day 77 theme: claiming a new wealthy family story. The prompts move from imagining what the rewritten story says, to sitting with who is doing the rewriting, to finding the specific strength in your ancestry that gave you the capacity to do it. Set a timer. Write without editing.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
If you could rewrite your family's entire relationship with money (past, present, and future), what would that story say?
Write the new story. Include the beliefs, the traditions, the values you would pass on. Be specific.
What beliefs, traditions, or patterns are you already passing on that are different from the ones you inherited?
Name the specific things. The conversations you are having, the decisions you are making differently, the relationship with money your children are seeing.
How does it feel to know that you are the person changing the lineage?
Write what the responsibility feels like, and what the pride feels like. Both are real.
Who or what in your ancestry gave you the strength to create this new story?
Name the person or the quality. Write what they gave you that made this moment possible. A thank you, plainly stated.
Adapted from the Day 77 journal prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
